task: Create and validate a premium editorial hero for the FrankX ChatGPT Sites founder guide.
surface: FrankX.ai long-form blog hero and Open Graph image
audience: Founders, operators, creators, and technical leaders evaluating AI-native website builders
first_read: A website becomes durable when intent moves through source, versioning, deployment, and audience controls.

references.label: FrankX visual system
references.reason: Defines the obsidian foundation, emerald/cyan technical spectrum, restrained glow, and editorial precision.

references.label: ChatGPT Sites lifecycle
references.reason: Grounds the visual metaphor in the documented project, saved-version, deployment, and audience lifecycle.

avoid: Generic node clouds and orbit graphics
Fake dashboards or illegible interface text
Decorative gradients without a concrete delivery metaphor
direction.signature: Five architectural gates convert a raw signal into one inspectable public surface.
system: No text, logos, people, or fake UI

asset_quality.why_premium: The custom art direction encodes the article's core operating model and is created specifically for the FrankX editorial surface.

artifacts.notes: Inspected at the original 1680x945 export. The article hero uses the same 16:9 ratio at desktop and mobile widths, preserving all five stages without an object-cover crop.

checks.name: asset-tier
checks.evidence: Tier B custom generated editorial media selected before production.

checks.name: export-inspection
checks.evidence: Original 1680x945 PNG inspected at full resolution: five stages are countable, geometry is clean, and no text, logos, people, or fake UI are present.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: The final asset and HeroImage component were inspected. Both use an exact 16:9 aspect ratio, so the complete sequence remains visible across desktop and mobile layouts; live-page verification remains part of release QA.

score.notes: First read 5, brand fit 5, craft 5, accessibility/responsiveness 4, provenance/artifact quality 5, usefulness 4.

known_risks: The fourth stage reads as a deployed architectural structure rather than a literal hosting control, so the surrounding article must carry the exact product semantics.
